# Model 2.2: Sample-Level Prediction of COVID Deterioration

This model predicts future COVID deterioration from the metabolomic and clinical profile available at a single sample/presentation.

Multiple samples from the same patient are allowed as separate rows because each sample represents a separate clinical presentation. To prevent patient-level leakage, grouped cross-validation is used so all samples from the same patient stay in the same fold.

```{r}
library(reticulate)

py_require(c(
  "pandas",
  "numpy", 
  "matplotlib",
  "seaborn",
  "scikit-learn",
  "xgboost",
  "joblib"
))
```

```{python}
# ============================================================
# SETUP
# ============================================================

import os
import hashlib
import joblib
import warnings

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

from sklearn.model_selection import GroupKFold, GridSearchCV, cross_validate, cross_val_predict
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.feature_selection import SelectFromModel

from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.ensemble import ExtraTreesClassifier
from sklearn.naive_bayes import GaussianNB
from sklearn.neural_network import MLPClassifier
from sklearn.svm import SVC
from xgboost import XGBClassifier

from sklearn.metrics import (
    classification_report,
    ConfusionMatrixDisplay,
    roc_curve,
    roc_auc_score,
    precision_recall_curve,
    average_precision_score,
    f1_score,
    balanced_accuracy_score,
    precision_score,
    recall_score
)

RANDOM_STATE = 42

CACHE_VERSION = "model_2_2"
CACHE_DIR = "cached_model_2_2"
os.makedirs(CACHE_DIR, exist_ok=True)

sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid")
```

# Load Data

```{python}
# ============================================================
# LOAD DATA
# ============================================================

df = pd.read_csv("model2dataset.csv")

df = df.replace(["-", "None", ""], np.nan)

df["day_from_sample"] = pd.to_numeric(df["day_from_sample"], errors="coerce")

df["time_from_symptom_onset_to_icu_days"] = pd.to_numeric(
    df["time_from_symptom_onset_to_icu_days"],
    errors="coerce"
)

df["x30_day_mortality"] = pd.to_numeric(
    df["x30_day_mortality"],
    errors="coerce"
).fillna(0)

df = df.drop(columns=[c for c in ["F1_X", "F2_X", "F3_X", "F4_X"] if c in df.columns])

print("Raw data shape:", df.shape)
print("Unique patients:", df["patient_id"].nunique())
```

# Create Sample-Level Future Deterioration Target

```{python}
# ============================================================
# SAMPLE-LEVEL TARGET
# ============================================================

df["sample_day"] = df["day_from_sample"]

def make_sample_target(row):
    icu_time = row["time_from_symptom_onset_to_icu_days"]
    sample_day = row["sample_day"]
    death_30 = row["x30_day_mortality"]

    if pd.isna(sample_day):
        return np.nan

    if death_30 == 1:
        return "deteriorating"

    if pd.notna(icu_time) and icu_time > sample_day:
        return "deteriorating"

    if pd.notna(icu_time) and icu_time <= sample_day:
        return np.nan

    return "stable"

df["deterioration_binary"] = df.apply(make_sample_target, axis=1)

df_model = df.dropna(subset=["deterioration_binary", "patient_id"]).copy()

print("Sample-level target counts:")
print(df_model["deterioration_binary"].value_counts())

print("\nPatients retained:")
print(df_model["patient_id"].nunique())
```

# Define Candidate Predictor Groups

```{python}
# ============================================================
# DEFINE CANDIDATE PREDICTOR GROUPS
# ============================================================

# This is NOT final feature selection.
# This only defines the candidate feature pool using dataset structure and clinical domain variables.
# Actual feature selection happens later inside the cross-validation pipeline.

metabolite_cols = [
    c for c in df_model.columns
    if c.startswith(("F1_", "F2_", "F3_", "F4_"))
]

clinical_candidate_names = {
    "sex",
    "bmi",
    "age",
    "age_at_symptom_onset_years",
    "crp",
    "d_dimer",
    "neutrophil_percent",
    "co2",
    "neutrophil_absolute",
    "lymphocyte_absolute",
    "lymphocyte_percent",
    "il1b",
    "il10",
    "il6",
    "rantes",
    "mip1a",
    "gmcsf",
    "mcp1",
    "il15",
    "hgf",
    "vegf",
    "il1ra",
    "il2r",
    "mig",
    "il8"
}

clinical_cols = [
    c for c in df_model.columns
    if c.lower() in clinical_candidate_names
]

time_cols = ["sample_day"]

feature_cols = clinical_cols + time_cols + metabolite_cols

print("Metabolite features:", len(metabolite_cols))
print("Clinical features:", len(clinical_cols))
print("Time features:", len(time_cols))
print("Total candidate predictors:", len(feature_cols))
```

# Clean Model Dataset

```{python}
# ============================================================
# CLEAN MODEL DATA
# ============================================================

target_col = "deterioration_binary"

model_df = df_model[["patient_id", target_col] + feature_cols].copy()

if "sex" in model_df.columns:
    model_df["sex"] = model_df["sex"].map({
        "Male": 1,
        "Female": 0,
        "male": 1,
        "female": 0,
        "M": 1,
        "F": 0
    })

for col in model_df.columns:
    if col not in ["patient_id", target_col]:
        model_df[col] = pd.to_numeric(model_df[col], errors="coerce")

feature_cols = [c for c in model_df.columns if c not in ["patient_id", target_col]]

missing_rate = model_df[feature_cols].isna().mean()
drop_missing = missing_rate[missing_rate > 0.50].index.tolist()

model_df = model_df.drop(columns=drop_missing)

feature_cols = [c for c in model_df.columns if c not in ["patient_id", target_col]]

constant_cols = [
    c for c in feature_cols
    if model_df[c].nunique(dropna=True) <= 1
]

model_df = model_df.drop(columns=constant_cols)

print("Final model dataframe shape:", model_df.shape)
print("Dropped high-missing features:", len(drop_missing))
print("Dropped constant features:", len(constant_cols))

print("\nFinal class counts:")
print(model_df[target_col].value_counts())

print("\nFinal unique patients:")
print(model_df["patient_id"].nunique())
```

# Prepare Modelling Objects

```{python}
# ============================================================
# PREPARE X, y, GROUPS
# ============================================================

X = model_df.drop(columns=["patient_id", target_col])
X.columns = X.columns.astype(str)

y_raw = model_df[target_col].astype(str)
groups = model_df["patient_id"].astype(str)

# Important:
# 0 = stable
# 1 = deteriorating
# This keeps probability thresholds intuitive.
y = (y_raw == "deteriorating").astype(int).to_numpy()

positive_class = 1
negative_class = 0

target_names = ["stable", "deteriorating"]

n_positive = np.sum(y == positive_class)
n_negative = np.sum(y == negative_class)

scale_pos_weight = n_negative / n_positive

print("Class mapping:")
print("stable -> 0")
print("deteriorating -> 1")

print("\nSamples:", X.shape[0])
print("Predictors:", X.shape[1])
print("Unique patient groups:", groups.nunique())

print("\nClass proportions:")
print(y_raw.value_counts(normalize=True))

print("\nXGBoost scale_pos_weight:", round(scale_pos_weight, 3))
```

# Class Distribution Plot

```{python}
# ============================================================
# CLASS DISTRIBUTION PLOT
# ============================================================

class_counts = y_raw.value_counts().reindex(["stable", "deteriorating"])

plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
sns.barplot(x=class_counts.index, y=class_counts.values)

plt.title("Model 2 Class Distribution")
plt.xlabel("Class")
plt.ylabel("Number of Samples")

for i, value in enumerate(class_counts.values):
    plt.text(i, value + 5, str(value), ha="center")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# Define Cache Key

```{python}
# ============================================================
# CACHE KEY
# ============================================================

cache_string = (
    CACHE_VERSION
    + str(X.shape)
    + str(list(X.columns))
    + str(pd.Series(y).value_counts().to_dict())
)

cache_key = hashlib.md5(cache_string.encode()).hexdigest()[:10]

print("Cache key:", cache_key)
```

# Feature Selection Object

```{python}
df_features = pd.read_csv("metabolite_selections.csv")
model2_features = df_features.loc[
    df_features["Dataset_Availability"].isin(["Both", "Model 2 only"]),
    "Model_2_Name"
].dropna()
model2_features = model2_features[model2_features.isin(X.columns)].tolist()
X_2 = X[model2_features]
```

```{python}
# ============================================================
# FEATURE SELECTION OBJECT
# ============================================================

# Tree-based feature selection is placed inside each pipeline.
# This means feature selection is performed separately inside each CV training fold,
# preventing feature-selection leakage.

max_selected_features = min(100, X_2.shape[1])
et_selector = SelectFromModel(
    ExtraTreesClassifier(
        n_estimators=250,
        class_weight="balanced",
        random_state=RANDOM_STATE,
        max_features="sqrt",
        min_samples_leaf=2,
        n_jobs=1
    ),
    max_features=max_selected_features
)
```

# Define Six Models

```{python}
# ============================================================
# SIX MODEL DEFINITIONS + HYPERPARAMETER GRIDS
# ============================================================

model_specs = {

    "Extra Trees": {
        "pipeline": Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
            ("feature_selection", et_selector),
            ("model", ExtraTreesClassifier(
                class_weight="balanced",
                random_state=RANDOM_STATE,
                n_jobs=1
            ))
        ]),
        "param_grid": {
            "feature_selection__max_features": [10, 20, 30, 40],
            "model__n_estimators": [250, 400],
            "model__max_features": ["sqrt", 0.3],
            "model__min_samples_leaf": [2, 3],
            "model__max_depth": [None, 8]
        }
    },

    "XGBoost": {
        "pipeline": Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
            ("feature_selection", et_selector),
            ("model", XGBClassifier(
                objective="binary:logistic",
                eval_metric="logloss",
                random_state=RANDOM_STATE,
                n_jobs=1,
                scale_pos_weight=scale_pos_weight
            ))
        ]),
        "param_grid": {
            "model__n_estimators": [150, 250],
            "model__max_depth": [2, 3],
            "model__learning_rate": [0.03, 0.05],
            "model__subsample": [0.8, 1.0],
            "model__colsample_bytree": [0.8, 1.0]
        }
    },

    "Elastic Net Logistic Regression": {
        "pipeline": Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
            ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
            ("feature_selection", et_selector),
            ("model", LogisticRegression(
                max_iter=5000,
                class_weight="balanced",
                penalty="elasticnet",
                solver="saga",
                random_state=RANDOM_STATE
            ))
        ]),
        "param_grid": {
            "feature_selection__max_features": [10, 20, 30, 40],
            "model__C": [0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1.0],
            "model__l1_ratio": [0.25, 0.5, 0.75]
        }
    },

    "SVM RBF": {
        "pipeline": Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
            ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
            ("feature_selection", et_selector),
            ("model", SVC(
                kernel="rbf",
                probability=True,
                class_weight="balanced",
                random_state=RANDOM_STATE
            ))
        ]),
        "param_grid": {
            "feature_selection__max_features": [10, 20, 30, 40],
            "model__C": [0.5, 1.0, 2.0],
            "model__gamma": ["scale", 0.01]
        }
    },

    "Gaussian Naive Bayes": {
        "pipeline": Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
            ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
            ("feature_selection", et_selector),
            ("model", GaussianNB())
        ]),
        "param_grid": {
            "feature_selection__max_features": [10, 20, 30, 40],
            "model__var_smoothing": [1e-9, 1e-8, 1e-7, 1e-6]
        }
    },

    "MLP Neural Network": {
        "pipeline": Pipeline([
            ("imputer", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
            ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
            ("feature_selection", et_selector),
            ("model", MLPClassifier(
                activation="relu",
                solver="adam",
                early_stopping=True,
                validation_fraction=0.2,
                n_iter_no_change=30,
                max_iter=1500,
                random_state=RANDOM_STATE
            ))
        ]),
        "param_grid": {
            "feature_selection__max_features": [10, 20, 30, 40],
            "model__hidden_layer_sizes": [
                (16,),
                (32,),
                (32, 16),
                (64, 32),
                (64, 32, 16)
            ],
            "model__alpha": [0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1],
            "model__learning_rate_init": [0.001, 0.0005, 0.0001],
            "model__batch_size": [16, 32, 64]
        }
    }
}

print("Models included:")
for model_name in model_specs.keys():
    print("-", model_name)
```

# Cached Hyperparameter Tuning

```{python}
# ============================================================
# CACHED GROUPED HYPERPARAMETER TUNING
# ============================================================

inner_cv = GroupKFold(n_splits=5)

tuned_results = {}
best_estimators = {}

for name, spec in model_specs.items():

    safe_name = name.replace(" ", "_").replace("/", "_")
    cache_path = os.path.join(CACHE_DIR, f"{cache_key}_{safe_name}_best_model.joblib")

    if os.path.exists(cache_path):
        print(f"\nLoading cached tuned model: {name}")

        cached = joblib.load(cache_path)

        best_estimators[name] = cached["best_estimator"]
        tuned_results[name] = cached["tuned_result"]

        continue

    print(f"\nTuning model: {name}")

    search = GridSearchCV(
        estimator=spec["pipeline"],
        param_grid=spec["param_grid"],
        scoring="f1_macro",
        cv=inner_cv,
        n_jobs=1,
        error_score="raise",
        refit=True
    )

    search.fit(X_2, y, groups=groups)

    tuned_results[name] = {
        "Best CV Macro F1": search.best_score_,
        "Best Params": search.best_params_
    }

    best_estimators[name] = search.best_estimator_

    joblib.dump({
        "best_estimator": search.best_estimator_,
        "tuned_result": tuned_results[name]
    }, cache_path)

    print("Best CV Macro F1:", round(search.best_score_, 3))
    print("Saved cache:", cache_path)

tuned_results_df = pd.DataFrame(tuned_results).T.sort_values(
    "Best CV Macro F1",
    ascending=False
)

print("\nHyperparameter tuning summary:")
print(tuned_results_df)
```

# Final Grouped Cross-Validated Model Comparison

```{python}
# ============================================================
# FINAL GROUPED CV MODEL COMPARISON
# ============================================================

outer_cv = GroupKFold(n_splits=5)

final_results_cache = os.path.join(CACHE_DIR, f"{cache_key}_final_results.joblib")

if os.path.exists(final_results_cache):

    print("Loading cached final CV results.")
    final_results_df = joblib.load(final_results_cache)

else:

    final_results = {}

    for name, estimator in best_estimators.items():

        print("\nEvaluating tuned model:", name)

        scores = cross_validate(
            estimator,
            X_2,
            y,
            groups=groups,
            cv=outer_cv,
            scoring={
                "balanced_accuracy": "balanced_accuracy",
                "macro_f1": "f1_macro",
                "auroc": "roc_auc",
                "precision": "precision",
                "recall": "recall"
            },
            n_jobs=1,
            error_score="raise"
        )

        final_results[name] = {
            "Balanced Accuracy Mean": scores["test_balanced_accuracy"].mean(),
            "Balanced Accuracy SD": scores["test_balanced_accuracy"].std(),
            "Macro F1 Mean": scores["test_macro_f1"].mean(),
            "Macro F1 SD": scores["test_macro_f1"].std(),
            "AUROC Mean": scores["test_auroc"].mean(),
            "AUROC SD": scores["test_auroc"].std(),
            "Precision Mean": scores["test_precision"].mean(),
            "Precision SD": scores["test_precision"].std(),
            "Recall Mean": scores["test_recall"].mean(),
            "Recall SD": scores["test_recall"].std()
        }

    final_results_df = pd.DataFrame(final_results).T.sort_values(
        "Macro F1 Mean",
        ascending=False
    )

    joblib.dump(final_results_df, final_results_cache)

print("\nFinal grouped-CV model comparison:")
print(final_results_df)
```

# Model Comparison Plots

```{python}
# ============================================================
# FOUR SEPARATE MODEL PERFORMANCE BOXPLOTS
# ============================================================

fold_rows = []

for name, estimator in best_estimators.items():

    scores = cross_validate(
        estimator,
        X_2,
        y,
        groups=groups,
        cv=outer_cv,
        scoring={
            "Macro F1": "f1_macro",
            "AUROC": "roc_auc",
            "Balanced Accuracy": "balanced_accuracy",
            "Deteriorating Recall": "recall"
        },
        n_jobs=1,
        error_score="raise"
    )

    for i in range(5):
        fold_rows.append({
            "Model": name,
            "Macro F1": scores["test_Macro F1"][i],
            "AUROC": scores["test_AUROC"][i],
            "Balanced Accuracy": scores["test_Balanced Accuracy"][i],
            "Deteriorating Recall": scores["test_Deteriorating Recall"][i]
        })

fold_df = pd.DataFrame(fold_rows)

rename_map = {
    "Elastic Net Logistic Regression": "Elastic Net",
    "MLP Neural Network": "MLP",
    "Extra Trees": "Extra Trees",
    "XGBoost": "XGBoost",
    "SVM RBF": "SVM",
    "Gaussian Naive Bayes": "Gaussian NB"
}

fold_df["Model"] = fold_df["Model"].map(rename_map)

model_order = [
    "Elastic Net",
    "MLP",
    "Extra Trees",
    "XGBoost",
    "SVM",
    "Gaussian NB"
]

palette = sns.color_palette("Set2", n_colors=len(model_order))
palette_dict = dict(zip(model_order, palette))

metrics = [
    "Macro F1",
    "AUROC",
    "Balanced Accuracy",
    "Deteriorating Recall"
]

for metric in metrics:

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6.5))

    sns.boxplot(
        data=fold_df,
        x="Model",
        y=metric,
        order=model_order,
        palette=palette_dict,
        width=0.6,
        linewidth=1.2
    )

    sns.stripplot(
        data=fold_df,
        x="Model",
        y=metric,
        order=model_order,
        color="black",
        size=5.5,
        alpha=0.55,
        jitter=0.12
    )

    plt.title(f"Grouped Cross-Validation {metric}", fontsize=17, fontweight="bold")
    plt.xlabel("")
    plt.ylabel("Score", fontsize=13)
    plt.ylim(0.55, 0.95)

    plt.xticks(rotation=20, ha="right", fontsize=11)
    plt.yticks(fontsize=11)

    plt.grid(axis="y", linestyle="--", alpha=0.35)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

# Select Best Model

```{python}
# ============================================================
# SELECT BEST MODEL
# ============================================================

best_model_name = final_results_df.index[0]
best_pipe = best_estimators[best_model_name]

print("Best final model:", best_model_name)

print("\nBest tuned parameters:")
print(tuned_results_df.loc[best_model_name, "Best Params"])
```

# Cross-Validated Predictions for Best Model

```{python}
# ============================================================
# CACHED CROSS-VALIDATED PREDICTIONS FOR BEST MODEL
# ============================================================

safe_best_name = best_model_name.replace(" ", "_").replace("/", "_")
pred_cache_path = os.path.join(CACHE_DIR, f"{cache_key}_{safe_best_name}_predictions.joblib")

if os.path.exists(pred_cache_path):

    print("Loading cached cross-validated predictions.")

    pred_cache = joblib.load(pred_cache_path)

    y_pred_cv = pred_cache["y_pred_cv"]
    y_proba_cv = pred_cache["y_proba_cv"]

else:

    print("Generating cross-validated predictions for best model.")

    y_pred_cv = cross_val_predict(
        best_pipe,
        X_2,
        y,
        groups=groups,
        cv=outer_cv,
        n_jobs=1,
        method="predict"
    )

    y_proba_cv = cross_val_predict(
        best_pipe,
        X_2,
        y,
        groups=groups,
        cv=outer_cv,
        n_jobs=1,
        method="predict_proba"
    )

    joblib.dump({
        "y_pred_cv": y_pred_cv,
        "y_proba_cv": y_proba_cv
    }, pred_cache_path)

positive_index = list(best_pipe.classes_).index(positive_class)
positive_probs = y_proba_cv[:, positive_index]
```

# Default Threshold Performance

```{python}
# ============================================================
# DEFAULT THRESHOLD PERFORMANCE
# ============================================================

print("Classification report using default threshold:")
print(classification_report(y, y_pred_cv, target_names=target_names))

auc_value = roc_auc_score(y, positive_probs)
avg_precision = average_precision_score(y, positive_probs)

print("Grouped-CV AUROC:", round(auc_value, 3))
print("Grouped-CV Average Precision:", round(avg_precision, 3))

ConfusionMatrixDisplay.from_predictions(
    y,
    y_pred_cv,
    display_labels=target_names,
    cmap="Blues"
)

plt.title(f"Default Threshold Confusion Matrix - {best_model_name}")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# Threshold Tuning

```{python}
# ============================================================
# THRESHOLD TUNING
# ============================================================

threshold_grid = np.arange(0.05, 0.96, 0.01)

threshold_rows = []

for threshold in threshold_grid:

    pred_labels = (positive_probs >= threshold).astype(int)

    threshold_rows.append({
        "threshold": threshold,
        "macro_f1": f1_score(y, pred_labels, average="macro"),
        "balanced_accuracy": balanced_accuracy_score(y, pred_labels),
        "deteriorating_precision": precision_score(y, pred_labels, zero_division=0),
        "deteriorating_recall": recall_score(y, pred_labels, zero_division=0),
        "stable_recall": recall_score(1 - y, 1 - pred_labels, zero_division=0)
    })

threshold_df = pd.DataFrame(threshold_rows)

best_threshold_row = threshold_df.sort_values("macro_f1", ascending=False).iloc[0]
best_threshold = best_threshold_row["threshold"]

print("Best threshold based on macro F1:")
print(best_threshold_row)

threshold_pred_labels = (positive_probs >= best_threshold).astype(int)

print("\nClassification report using tuned threshold:")
print(classification_report(
    y,
    threshold_pred_labels,
    target_names=target_names
))

ConfusionMatrixDisplay.from_predictions(
    y,
    threshold_pred_labels,
    display_labels=target_names,
    cmap="Blues"
)

plt.title(f"Tuned Threshold Confusion Matrix - {best_model_name} - Threshold {best_threshold:.2f}")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# Threshold Sensitivity Plot

```{python}
# ============================================================
# THRESHOLD SENSITIVITY PLOT
# ============================================================

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))

plt.plot(threshold_df["threshold"], threshold_df["macro_f1"], label="Macro F1")
plt.plot(threshold_df["threshold"], threshold_df["balanced_accuracy"], label="Balanced Accuracy")
plt.plot(threshold_df["threshold"], threshold_df["deteriorating_precision"], label="Deteriorating Precision")
plt.plot(threshold_df["threshold"], threshold_df["deteriorating_recall"], label="Deteriorating Recall")

plt.axvline(best_threshold, linestyle="--", label=f"Best threshold = {best_threshold:.2f}")

plt.xlabel("Decision Threshold for Deteriorating Class")
plt.ylabel("Score")
plt.title("Threshold Sensitivity Analysis")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# ROC Curve

```{python}
# ============================================================
# ROC CURVE
# ============================================================

fpr, tpr, roc_thresholds = roc_curve(y, positive_probs)

plt.figure(figsize=(6, 5))

plt.plot(fpr, tpr, label=f"AUC = {auc_value:.3f}")
plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], linestyle="--")

plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.title(f"Grouped-CV ROC Curve - {best_model_name}")

plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# Precision-Recall Curve

```{python}
# ============================================================
# PRECISION-RECALL CURVE
# ============================================================

precision, recall, pr_thresholds = precision_recall_curve(y, positive_probs)

plt.figure(figsize=(6, 5))

plt.plot(recall, precision, label=f"Average Precision = {avg_precision:.3f}")

plt.xlabel("Recall")
plt.ylabel("Precision")
plt.title(f"Grouped-CV Precision-Recall Curve - {best_model_name}")

plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# Probability Distribution Plot

```{python}
# ============================================================
# PROBABILITY DISTRIBUTION PLOT
# ============================================================

prob_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Predicted probability of deterioration": positive_probs,
    "True class": np.where(y == 1, "deteriorating", "stable")
})

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))

sns.kdeplot(
    data=prob_df,
    x="Predicted probability of deterioration",
    hue="True class",
    fill=True,
    common_norm=False,
    alpha=0.4
)

plt.axvline(best_threshold, linestyle="--", label=f"Tuned threshold = {best_threshold:.2f}")

plt.title("Predicted Probability Distribution by True Class")
plt.xlabel("Predicted probability of deterioration")
plt.ylabel("Density")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# Feature Importance

```{python}
# ============================================================
# FEATURE IMPORTANCE
# ============================================================

best_pipe.fit(X_2, y)

selector = best_pipe.named_steps["feature_selection"]
model = best_pipe.named_steps["model"]

selected_features = X_2.columns[selector.get_support()]

if hasattr(model, "feature_importances_"):
    importance_values = model.feature_importances_

elif hasattr(model, "coef_"):
    importance_values = np.mean(np.abs(model.coef_), axis=0)

else:
    importance_values = np.repeat(np.nan, len(selected_features))

importance_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "feature": selected_features,
    "importance": importance_values
}).sort_values("importance", ascending=False)

print("Top selected predictors:")
print(importance_df.head(30))

top_n = 15

plot_importance = importance_df.head(top_n).copy()

plot_importance["short_feature"] = [
    f[:38] + "..." if len(f) > 38 else f
    for f in plot_importance["feature"]
]

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 7))

plt.barh(
    plot_importance["short_feature"][::-1],
    plot_importance["importance"][::-1]
)

plt.xlabel("Importance")
plt.ylabel("")
plt.title(f"Top {top_n} Selected Predictors - {best_model_name}")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# Save Report Tables

```{python}
# ============================================================
# SAVE REPORT TABLES
# ============================================================

final_results_df.to_csv("model2_grouped_cv_results.csv")
tuned_results_df.to_csv("model2_tuned_hyperparameters.csv")
threshold_df.to_csv("model2_threshold_sensitivity.csv", index=False)
importance_df.to_csv("model2_feature_importance.csv", index=False)

print("Saved report tables:")
print("- model2_grouped_cv_results.csv")
print("- model2_tuned_hyperparameters.csv")
print("- model2_threshold_sensitivity.csv")
print("- model2_feature_importance.csv")
```

# Final Summary

```{python}
# ============================================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
# ============================================================

print("================ FINAL SUMMARY ================")

print("Aim: Predict future deterioration from one sample/presentation")
print("Leakage control: GroupKFold by patient_id")

print("Samples:", X_2.shape[0])
print("Unique patients:", groups.nunique())
print("Predictors before selection:", X_2.shape[1])

print("Best model:", best_model_name)

print("Grouped-CV AUROC:", round(auc_value, 3))
print("Grouped-CV Average Precision:", round(avg_precision, 3))

print("Default threshold Macro F1:", round(f1_score(y, y_pred_cv, average="macro"), 3))
print("Best tuned threshold:", round(best_threshold, 2))
print("Tuned threshold Macro F1:", round(f1_score(y, threshold_pred_labels, average="macro"), 3))

print("\nBest model grouped CV performance:")
print(final_results_df.loc[[best_model_name]].to_string())

print("================================================")
```

# Interpretation Notes

The final model treats each blood sample as a separate clinical presentation, allowing multiple time points from the same patient to contribute separate observations. Grouped cross-validation was used so all samples from the same patient were kept in the same fold, preventing patient-level leakage.

The modelling workflow compares six distinct modelling paradigms: tree ensembles, gradient boosting, sparse linear modelling, kernel methods, probabilistic classification, and neural networks. Candidate clinical and metabolomic predictors are first defined using dataset structure and domain relevance, while final feature selection occurs inside the machine learning pipeline using tree-based selection within cross-validation.

The best model should be interpreted using AUROC, average precision, macro F1, balanced accuracy, and class-specific recall rather than accuracy alone, because the clinical problem involves imbalance and the deteriorating class is the class of interest.
